metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified the scale of the problem on the 10 Apr 2026 discovery call: indirect spend of roughly £650m/year across about 2,000 live suppliers, of which she estimates 80% sits in unmanaged tail spend, plus direct spend of roughly £2bn. She referenced a publicly announced £80m synergy target tied to the Bakkavor acquisition but could not confirm how much is allocated to procurement. Zycus committed on that same call to follow up with a savings/business-case outline 'today or Monday' (i.e. by roughly 13 Apr 2026); there is no evidence in the record that this was delivered, so the business case remains unquantified from Zycus's side.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad is personally invested and driving the opportunity ('I will be running the project... I'm deeply invested... I'm a big fan') and has looped in James Dean as a co-lead. But she herself conceded on 10 Apr 2026 that internal power is not yet secured ('the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet'), and per the 15 May 2026 update she has since raised a new, material concern about her own team's bandwidth to run an implementation at all. She reads as a developing champion with genuine intent but unproven access to power.

competition.narrative: The current point-solution landscape is well understood from the 10 Apr 2026 call: Scanmarket for eProcurement, DocuSign for the contract repository, Click for spend visibility (directs only), Pactum for AI-assisted negotiation (in its second year of live use and, per Rachael, mentioned 'at every possible opportunity'), and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she says she loves. None of these is a full source-to-pay suite rival, and Rachael was explicit that no formal vendor shortlist exists yet; the real competitive field only forms once the RFP launches, which she now targets for end of 2026.

identify_pain.narrative: Rachael Coad gave a detailed, first-hand account of the pain on 10 Apr 2026: roughly seven disparate ERP systems following the Bakkavor acquisition (13 sites on legacy Infor System 21, others on Great Plains and Microsoft Dynamics 365, plus a 5-year SAP S/4HANA migration underway), a contract repository (DocuSign) that 'doesn't tie into anything,' an eProcurement tool (Scanmarket) similarly siloed, zero purchase-order visibility for procurement ('I've never seen a purchase order'), and roughly 80% of 2,000 live suppliers sitting in unmanaged tail spend with no onboarding risk process ('they are not managed... I mean they are not managed'). This is specific, buyer-articulated, and reasonably recent.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process exists. The deal is pre-RFP and pre-budget-approval, which is expected and not itself a red flag at Qualified stage, but there is genuinely no MSA/Order Form/SOW conversation on record.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged. Rachael Coad said plainly on 10 Apr 2026, 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet,' and that part of her job is to build a compelling business case for unnamed senior leadership before any purchase can be pressed forward ('I don't want to go to market first, there is a lot of work to be done'). The 15 May 2026 on-site update again names only Rachael and James Dean as engaged; no budget holder or C-level sponsor has surfaced on the buyer side.

decision_process.narrative: The process Rachael described on 10 Apr 2026 is sequential and buyer-controlled: build the business case first, secure leadership sign-off, then run a formal RFP she now targets for 'end of 2026' per the 15 May 2026 next-step note (roughly 5 months from today, 9 Jul 2026). This is understood narratively but has no artifact trail yet (no confirmed exchange of the RFP primer or savings model).

decision_criteria.narrative: Zycus committed on 10 Apr 2026 to share an RFP primer document to help Rachael 'build out a list of requirements' ahead of the demo; there is no confirmation the primer was delivered. Rachael did verbally specify demo-shaping criteria ('give us specific use cases... it has to be interactive, live'), and the 15 May 2026 next-step note names target capability areas: AI, savings-opportunity analysis, contract management, and supplier risk management. Formal, documented evaluation criteria do not yet exist because the RFP itself has not launched.

engagement.top_event: Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) unaddressed and due before BRD fit session on 17 June. These are table stakes for Vendor Selected gate.

critical_signals.text: At the on-site demo on 15 May 2026 (~8 weeks ago), the buyer's genuine enthusiasm for the platform was undercut by a new, self-identified blocker: she does not have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project right now.

The buyer already has AI tools live in production that she is emotionally attached to, an autonomous negotiation tool in its second year of use and a contract-redlining tool she says she loves, so Zycus is shaping the business case against incumbents the buyer likes, not a blank slate.

The buyer needs a quantified savings and business-case model to take to her own leadership before any RFP can launch, and there is no evidence Zycus delivered the one it committed to on 10 April 2026.

No pricing, budget owner, or commercial conversation exists yet; the buyer has been explicit that this is a long-play deal being kept warm until she is ready to run a formal RFP toward the end of 2026.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is genuinely progressing (discovery call 10 Apr, on-site demo 15 May, champion established 12 May, buyer email received 2 Jun, SF activity 23 Jun) but the 2026-04-12 close date is not credible: it was actually pulled IN by a year (from 2027-04-12 to 2026-04-12) on 2026-05-12, the same day Amount tripled from £150k to £450k, and Rachael Coad has stated on-call she plans to issue an RFP only toward end of 2026 — meaning a realistic close sits in 2027, not a date that has already passed.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Keep Pipeline category (appropriate for an unbudgeted, pre-RFP deal) but reset CloseDate off the placeholder 2026-04-12 to reflect the buyer-stated RFP timeline (end of 2026) — realistic close is Q1-Q2 2027.

flags: Competitive shortlist is latent but will surface at end-2026 RFP. Rachael confirmed multi-vendor evaluation underway but named no rivals. Zycus is positioned to shape the RFP criteria and business case before competition formally engages, which is a strong influence position. Risk: lose shaping advantage if competitors build relationships or business case support in parallel. Next move: ask Rachael who else is in her pipeline and what concerns or gaps she sees with them, then wedge Merlin AI (she already uses Pactum, proving AI appetite) and SAP partnership as differentiators in the business case.

Champion is single-threaded (Rachael + James only) with no economic buyer mapped: the 8 Jun executive-connect (Anthony Gray to the relevant stakeholder/CPO) is the top lever to unblock the resource/budget concern before competitors engage senior leadership.
Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) are overdue: deliver them now to keep the champion armed for the business case while engagement is warm.

Reset the close date to ~Q1 2027 to match the buyer-stated end-of-2026 RFP and the 'long play' framing; the current 12 Apr 2026 date is a placeholder already 79 days past and distorts forecast hygiene.

Deal is single-threaded on Rachael Coad and James Dean with no economic buyer or senior-leadership contact engaged; rivals could build parallel exec relationships before the RFP launches, so multi-threading via Rachael's own planned team build is the highest-leverage lever now.

Zycus is shaping the RFP itself via the primer sent 18 May 2026, a strong influence position ahead of the end-2026 RFP; convert that into named must-have criteria (agentic AI, tail-spend automation, one-data-core architecture) before the buyer finalizes scope.
best_practice_check.summary: The highest-leverage next moves are closing out Zycus's own open commitments, multi-threading beyond two contacts, and neutralising the resourcing objection before it hardens.

customer_preference.evidence: Rachael Coad showed specific, unprompted technical interest ('I'd be interested to understand a bit more about that savings opportunity agent') and voiced broad appetite for AI tools (10 Apr 2026), but has not stated any vendor preference versus a competitive shortlist, which does not yet exist.

competitive_position.summary: No full-suite S2P rival is currently in an active bake-off; the real competitive shortlist will only form once the buyer launches her RFP, targeted for end of 2026. Today's competitive exposure is a set of point tools the buyer already uses and likes (an AI negotiation tool, an AI redlining tool) alongside legacy, siloed systems (Scanmarket, DocuSign, Click) that Zycus is positioned to consolidate.

competitors.quote: we're in our second year of having it now... Pactum
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin ANA as a native negotiation agent embedded in a full source-to-pay platform rather than a standalone tool, offering to extend rather than displace what she already values.

competitors.name: Unnamed AI contract-redlining tool
competitors.quote: we've recently got a separate tool for contract red-light AI contract redlining, which I love. It is the best thing ever.
competitors.how_we_win: Show iContract's redlining plus full CLM lifecycle (repository, renewals, obligations) as a superset of a point tool she already loves, not a forced replacement.

competitors.name: Scanmarket
competitors.quote: we've got Scanmarket for eProcurement... it's a separate system that doesn't tie into anything
competitors.how_we_win: Show eProcurement unified on the same data core as sourcing and contracts, closing the indirect-spend gap Rachael already flagged as weak.

competitors.name: DocuSign CLM
competitors.quote: We're using Docusign as our repository. Docusign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Show iContract as a unified CLM tied into the same data core as sourcing and contracts, versus a disconnected document repository.

competitors.name: Full S2P competitive shortlist (to form at end-2026 RFP)
competitors.quote: Rachael Coad has named no specific full-suite rival; the real shortlist forms only once the RFP launches, which she now targets for end of 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Maintain the current shaping/advisor position (RFP primer, business-case support) so Zycus is embedded in requirements before rival full-suite vendors gain access to the RFP.

competitors.name: Click
competitors.quote: Click works well for directs, not well for indirects
competitors.how_we_win: Target the indirect spend-visibility gap Click does not cover as a clean, low-friction Zycus wedge.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early, well-articulated but single-threaded Qualified-stage deal. Genuine buyer-driven pain and engagement exist (a detailed, quantified discovery call and a customised on-site demo the buyer herself requested), but there is no economic buyer, no budget, and a newly surfaced resourcing objection, all of which cap the read at an early-stage level regardless of the strong rapport.

deal_risk.text: Close date already 88 days past (12 Apr 2026) while the buyer describes this as a 'long play' with RFP not expected until end of 2026 — the forecast date is not defensible.
Two Zycus commitments (RFP primer, savings/business-case model) made on 10 Apr 2026 have no confirmed delivery on record, risking loss of shaping influence ahead of the RFP.

win_position.text: Buyer-led, detailed discovery — Rachael Coad quantified spend (£650m indirect, £2bn direct, 80% tail unmanaged) unprompted on 10 Apr 2026, and personally requested and helped shape a customised on-site demo delivered 15 May 2026.
No economic buyer engaged — Rachael stated on 10 Apr 2026 that 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet' and that budget sign-off has not started.
Single-threaded — only Rachael Coad and James Dean are confirmed engaged; no wider stakeholder has surfaced since April.

deal_momentum.text: Recent two-way buyer contact — Greencore replied by email on 2 Jun 2026 (~37 days ago), and Salesforce shows activity as recently as 23 Jun 2026 (~16 days ago).
No near-term milestone until the October 2026 EY event (~3 months out) and the buyer's own RFP target of end of 2026 (~5 months out) — the cadence between real events is wide.

customer_commitment.text: Rachael personally committed to sourcing use cases and attendee lists for the demo, and delivered on that commitment (the 15 May 2026 demo happened as she arranged).
Rachael flagged on 15 May 2026 that she lacks internal headcount/bandwidth to run an implementation programme, a self-imposed constraint on her own commitment level to act quickly.
